description: Expanded Indo-European comparative mini-corpus: numbers 1–10, body parts, core verbs, across Latin, Greek, Sanskrit, Gothic, Old Irish, OCS, Lithuanian, Hittite, Tocharian B, plus scholarly PIE. Forms from published handbooks; uncertain/disputed/analogical/borrowed items flagged. Each item embeds a Dantomax-shaped HistoricalAttestation.
references: Benjamin W. Fortson IV. 2010. Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
Robert S. P. Beekes. 2011. Comparative Indo-European Linguistics: An Introduction.
Calvert Watkins. 2011. The American Heritage Dictionary of Indo-European Roots.
Friedrich Kluge / Elmar Seebold. 2011. Etymologisches Wörterbuch der deutschen Sprache.
Ranko Matasović. 2009. Etymological Dictionary of Proto-Celtic.
Rick Derksen. 2008. Etymological Dictionary of the Slavic Inherited Lexicon.
Sara E. Kimball. 1999. Hittite Historical Phonology.
Douglas Q. Adams. 2013. A Dictionary of Tocharian B.
